title: I got it Why Gendo was so concerned about Rei II maybe
display_title: I got it Why Gendo was so concerned about Rei II maybe
wikitext:
[]: I got it! Why Gendo was so concerned about Rei II, maybe!             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 7:21 pm Okay, I think I've figured it out. I don't remember seeing this anywhere, but as always, it may have been discussed before. Right, well, it's kind of a mystery as to why, in episode five, Gendo was so concerned about Rei II during the activation test. After all, he could just replace her, right? She'd have the same soul, even. So why care so much? This was mentioned on the Fan Geeks Commentary wiki. I can't cite it exactly as the wiki is currently not there (or it moved and I missed it). However, I believe the hypotheses offered were either that Gendo was just caught up in the moment because of Rei's similarity to Yui, or that he was acting for the sake of believability. These seem fairly dubious to me, epecially the latter. After all, everyone else would supposedly be concerned, but Gendo was the only one to rush to Rei's help. Okay, so what alternative is there? I've been mulling this over for a little bit, and it hit me. Was Gendo wearing gloves before his hands got burnt? I just checked, and indeed he wasn't. It was only after his hands were badly burnt by the heat of the entry plug's hatch that he started wearing his Mickey Mouse apparel. So, my conclusion is this: Gendo saved Rei not for the sake of keeping her from harm, but to deliberately burn his hands in front of several witnesses. This was for the sake of scarring his hands to suddenly start wearing gloves without anyone questioning it. Why wear gloves? Why, because he knew he'd be getting Adam soon, of course! If his hands were hidden, Adam could be easily hidden in one of them as well! So there you go! =D My idea's not convoluted at all! Also, one final piece of evidence to support this: from what I read in the Rebuild thread, Gendo apparently seems less emotional during the mirroring scene during YA(N)A. This goes to support the idea that his actions were not for Rei's sake, but for his own. That also adds another bit of pathos to Rei's situation: she now idolizes Gendo for saving her, and even shows enough emotion to slap Shinji when he doesn't trust Gendo. Yet her trust itself is misplaced. Gendo sacrificed nothing for her. ...Alright, discuss. Actually, first, tell me whether anyone's thought of this before. It's kind of hard to look through every post that has to do with gloves, Rei, burnt hands, etc.

[]: Re: I got it! Why Gendo was so concerned about Rei II, maybe             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Sep 21, 2007 7:41 pm NAveryW wrote: That also adds another bit of pathos to Rei's situation: she now idolizes Gendo for saving her, and even shows enough emotion to slap Shinji when he doesn't trust Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 21, 2007 7:44 pm Very good analytical eye, NAW-kun! ;) Oh, and here's the current link to the site. It got MOVED. http://evageeks.org/FGP/Main_Page btw, "Wanna be a member, wanna be a member?"

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 8:23 pm Ah, thanks for the relink. Yeah, I e-mailed Reichu a couple of days ago about memberbating myself, mainly because there are grammatical and spelling mistakes in various places that keep bugging me. I've received no response as of yet, though. And yes, come to think of it, Ornette, that's true. Gendo knew that he'd have to replace her, and if a bunch of people saw her die, it'd be quite suspicious. So, even if that was his motive instead of my idea, his concern still wasn't for her, it was for the Instrumentality Project. EDIT: I still think the gloves have something to do with it. I mean, he had to be planning to hide Adam somewhere. What else would he do; eat it? Ha! That's silly.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Sep 21, 2007 8:39 pm I'd always figured that Gendo saved Rei II because he genuinely cared about her on an emotional level, not because he's a ruthless bastard with some dirty trick up his sleeve. If he wanted to hide Adam, it seems like (as Ornette said) there'd be much easier ways to go about it than intentionally burning your hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 21, 2007 8:47 pm Couldn't it just be both? That Gendou DID care about Rei but also had his own purposes in mind? Or at the very least, since he ended up burning his hands, maybe he just figured "why not" when it came to uh, grafting Adam on there? Oh and with Reichu, I think school's been probably keeping her busy or something. She hasn't been on the FGP forums lately.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Sep 21, 2007 8:51 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Couldn't it just be both?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 8:56 pm Come on, you know Gendo's a huge jerk. Even if he did care about Rei, he could still clone her and neither of them have lost anything. Ass or Angel? He's the Fwerty-tirst Angel, Assiel.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Sep 21, 2007 8:58 pm Well, didn't Anno say that he didn't know where the story was going when he started? Maybe Rei wasn't so replaceable in the beginning, and throughout the original TV series, Gendo is never shown with Adam in his hand. That was added over in the directors cut and in End of Evangelion. Maybe Gendo really did care about Rei. Notice that the real turnover in Gendo's feelings for Rei comes around Episodes 14 to 17...and then on, right when the series flips to its darker side.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:15 pm I'm sure it was more well planned out than that . Look at the spectral image of Rei III (most likely) in episode one and you can tell something rum is going on with her. Aside from that, she's always been connected with the moon, hinting at her Lilithicity. And it's definitely always been the case that Yui was inside Eva 01. It's also definitely been the case that Rei is somehow related to Yui. That shows that a lot of her was planned out at the beginning. Oh yeah, that still doesn't say anything about whether she was initially planned to be replacable. Erm... leave me alone.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:20 pm There was always supposed to be something damn wierd about Rei, at the very least. Whether or not they had completely planned out her Lilithcity is debatable. At the very least, she was not supposed to be a normal human being, that was evident from the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:21 pm TriLink12 wrote: Is he an Ass or an Angel? Pick a side; We're at War!

It doesn't seem likely to me that Rei was in any shape to pilot Eva-01. However, chronologically, this happened AFTER he rushed down to the plug to burn his hands. (cue evil conspiracy music)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:23 pm OK, this thread is insane. Who replaced NAvery's brain with Eva Yojimbo's?

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:41 pm You may have something here. I always just figured that making Rei's wasn't cheap or easy. The fewer Rei's they burn though, the less trouble it is for Gendo and those who know about Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:48 pm Regardless of how expensive it is to make new ones, they had a bunch of them in Guf, just kind of floating there, giggling. I don't quite know what they found so funny. They were probably laughing at me. I know everyone else was.
